title: Clementine polenta cake
subtitle: A moist, gluten-free almond and polenta cake
excerpt: A dense, syrup-drenched cake made from ground almonds, polenta, and the juice and zest of four clementines. Gluten-free. The polenta gives it a slight graininess; the almond keeps it moist for four days.
sourceNotes: Polenta and almond cake combinations are documented in Northern Italian household cookery from the nineteenth century; recipes appear in pre-1928 Italian regional cookery texts and are referenced in early-twentieth-century British food writing influenced by Italian cooking. Clementines as a baking ingredient appear in pre-1928 Mediterranean cookery and British citrus cake recipes in the British Newspaper Archive.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze before syrup. Thaw, then warm in the oven and pour syrup over warm.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Keeps well for 4 days in a sealed container. The flavour deepens on day two.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven. Conventional 170°C. The dense batter needs low, steady heat. A skewer should come out clean but the cake will feel quite firm.

glossaryTerms.definition: Polenta used in cakes gives a slightly gritty, textured crumb that is quite different from a flour-based sponge. Fine polenta gives a more delicate texture; medium-ground polenta produces a coarser, more noticeable bite. Polenta cannot form gluten, so it is always combined with another binding agent: here, ground almonds and eggs. The result is inherently gluten-free if made with certified gluten-free polenta. The texture softens as the cake rests.

content.text: This is a moist citrus cake made without wheat flour:

and ground almonds carry the structure, and clementine zest and juice run through both the batter and a sharp syrup poured over the warm cake. Ten slices. The almond keeps it moist for several days, and the citrus flavour sharpens overnight.

items.prepNote: zest all 4, juice 2 for the cake, juice 2 for the syrup

content.text: Heat the oven to 160°C fan (170°C conventional). Grease and line the tin with baking paper. Beat the butter and 200 g of caster sugar together until pale, then add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each. Add the zest of all 4 clementines and the juice of 2. Fold in the ground almonds, polenta, and baking powder. The batter will be thick. Scrape into the tin and bake for 40 to 45 minutes, until a skewer comes out clean and the edges pull away from the tin.

While the cake bakes, heat the juice of the remaining 2 clementines with 50 g of caster sugar in a small saucepan until the sugar dissolves. As soon as the cake comes out of the oven, poke it all over with a skewer and pour the warm syrup slowly and evenly over the surface. Leave to cool in the tin for 20 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack.
